description_long: This object is a historical biplane with a distinctive canard configuration, featuring two large, fabric-covered wings arranged in a forward-swept V-shape. The wings are braced with numerous struts and wires, giving the structure a lightweight yet rigid appearance. The fuselage is minimal, with an open framework and exposed wooden and metal structural elements, and the cockpit is positioned at the front, open to the air. The tail assembly is located at the front of the aircraft, consisting of horizontal stabilizers and rudders, which is characteristic of early aviation designs. The landing gear consists of simple skids and small wheels, suitable for unpaved surfaces. The overall color scheme is a combination of off-white fabric, dark framework, and some wooden accents.
description: A vintage biplane with a forward-swept V-shaped wing, open cockpit, and exposed framework, featuring a canard tail and simple landing gear.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the V-shaped wings, open cockpit, and exposed framework with visible struts and wires.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical V-shaped wings, central cockpit, and canard tail assembly at the front.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view emphasizing the wing structure, open framework, and the canard tail at the front.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the full span of the wings and the open framework, with the canard tail visible at the front.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, forward-swept biplane with pronounced dihedral angle and extensive external bracing; no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single visible pusher propeller engine mounted at the rear of the cockpit framework.
attribute_tail_design: Canard configuration with forward-mounted horizontal stabilizers and twin vertical rudders.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed skid-type landing gear with small wheels, two main units visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit with single-seat configuration and no enclosure.
